title: Variational Autoencoders (VAE)
summary: Adds a probabilistic twist to autoencoders: the bottleneck is a distribution (mean + variance), not a point. Enables generation of new samples by sampling from the learned distribution. The loss combines reconstruction + KL divergence to the prior. Bridges autoencoders and generative models.
key_insights: Encoder outputs μ and σ (mean and variance of a Gaussian), not a single point z
z is sampled: z = μ + σ · ε where ε ~ N(0,1) — the 'reparameterization trick' enables gradient flow through sampling
Loss = reconstruction + β · KL(q(z|x) || p(z)) — forces the learned distribution to stay close to a prior (standard normal)
Can generate new data by sampling z ~ N(0,I) and decoding — the generative capability
Latent space is smooth and continuous (unlike basic autoencoders which can have gaps)

mathematical_core.encoder: q(z|x) = N(μ(x), σ²(x)) — encoder outputs distribution parameters
mathematical_core.reparameterization: z = μ + σ ⊙ ε, ε ~ N(0,I) — enables backprop through sampling
mathematical_core.loss: L = E_q[log p(x|z)] - KL(q(z|x) || p(z)) — ELBO (evidence lower bound)
mathematical_core.kl_gaussian: KL(N(μ,σ²) || N(0,1)) = -½ Σ(1 + log σ² - μ² - σ²)

related: FOUNDATIONS.INDUCTIVE_BIAS — VAE's Gaussian assumption is an inductive bias
